g.data-narration: Apache Oozie is a workflow scheduler system that manages and coordinates Hadoop jobs in complex data pipelines.
g.text: Workflow Scheduler for Hadoop & IoT Data Pipelines

g.data-narration: Oozie orchestrates multi-step data processing pipelines, handling job dependencies and scheduling for large-scale analytics.
g.text: A server-based workflow scheduling system that manages Apache Hadoop jobs.
Workflows are collections of actions arranged in a control dependency DAG.
Used in IoT for: Data Ingestion → Cleaning → Transformation → Analysis → Reporting

g.data-narration: Oozie provides workflow management, scheduling, Hadoop integration, scalability, and fault tolerance for data pipelines.
Oozie has three main abstractions: Workflows for job sequences, Coordinators for scheduling, and Bundles for group management.
A workflow is defined in XML with a start node, action nodes with ok/error transitions, kill node, and end node.
Control nodes manage workflow execution flow: start, end, kill, decision, fork, and join nodes define the workflow structure.
Action nodes execute Hadoop jobs: MapReduce, Pig, Hive, Sqoop, Shell, Java, and Spark integrate with Oozie workflows.
Decision nodes provide conditional branching using switch-case-default expressions for dynamic workflow paths based on conditions.
Fork nodes split execution into parallel paths, while join nodes synchronize all parallel branches before continuing the workflow.
Coordinators schedule workflows based on time (cron-like) or data availability triggers for recurring job execution.
g.text: Use Cases: Daily sensor aggregation | Hourly anomaly detection | Real-time alert generation

g.data-narration: Bundles group multiple coordinators for unified lifecycle management, enabling start/stop of related workflows together.
Real IoT pipelines include data ingestion, cleaning, parallel analysis, insights generation, and database export for reporting.
Oozie provides command-line tools for submitting, monitoring, suspending, resuming, and killing workflow jobs.
Oozie offers workflow orchestration, scheduling, and fault tolerance, but has verbose XML configuration and a learning curve.
g.text: • Basic web UI compared to modern tools

g.data-narration: Key takeaways: Oozie manages Hadoop workflows through Workflows (DAGs), Coordinators (scheduling), and Bundles (grouping).
g.text: 1. Workflow: DAG of actions with ok/error paths
2. Control Nodes: start, end, kill, decision, fork, join
3. Action Nodes: MR, Pig, Hive, Sqoop, Shell, Spark
